title: fraxinus americana var. alba
intro: Fraxinus americana var. alba, also known as White Ash, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America, characterized by its white or pale gray bark and compound leaves with 7-13 leaflets. The medicinal properties of White Ash include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its high content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of White Ash include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antiseptic, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of White Ash typically involve infusing its bark or leaves in hot water to create a tea, or using its extracts in tinctures or salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
